description: A French dish of edible snails, often baked in shells or special dishes with garlic-herb butter. The flavor is usually shaped more by the butter, garlic, and parsley than by the snail itself.
preparation_steps: Clean and prepare the snails if not already prepared.
Place the snails in shells or an escargot dish and add the butter.
Serve immediately, usually with bread for the sauce.

health_advice.benefits: Snails provide protein and minerals, while portion sizes are usually small.
health_advice.risks: The dish can be high in butter and sodium depending on preparation.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Enjoy in moderate portions and balance it with lighter items in the meal.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally low in carbohydrates, but bread often served alongside contains gluten.

allergens: Possible gluten if breadcrumbs are added
